cards.title: Create a Parenting Rota to Reduce Household Conflict
cards.body: A couple's simple system for dividing parenting duties transformed their relationship after their second child's birth. Moving away from blame and confusion, they established clear expectations that reduced bickering and overwhelm.

cards.title: Managing In-Law Relationships Before Having Children
cards.body: If a partner's parent displays cruelty, it's wise to plan boundaries before introducing grandchildren. Minimizing contact unless behavior changes significantly can protect your future family.

cards.title: Structure Over Pressure at Mealtime With Children
cards.body: Rather than becoming the food police, focus on providing consistent mealtime structure. This approach helps address eating concerns without pressuring children.

cards.title: The Mother-Daughter Dynamic Through Life's Transitions
cards.body: As daughters navigate their twenties and thirties, mothers can trust that the woman they're becoming is already emerging. Understanding this dynamic eases the beautiful chaos of these years.

cards.title: Sharenting: Weighing the Pros and Cons Online
cards.body: Parents frequently share their children's lives on social media, but this trend carries both benefits and risks. Understanding these tradeoffs helps parents make informed sharenting decisions.

cards.title: Perinatal Mental Health Often Goes Unrecognized
cards.body: Depression and other mental health concerns during pregnancy and postpartum are frequently underreported and under-treated. Recognizing symptoms and risk factors helps mothers access critical support services.

cards.title: Smacking Children Linked to Lower Academic Performance
cards.body: A University College London study found that physical punishment offers no benefits to children's development. Research suggests smacking may negatively impact educational outcomes.

cards.title: Ryanair Under Investigation for Family Seating Charges
cards.body: The UK's competition regulator is investigating the airline's practice of charging parents to sit next to their children. This policy raises questions about fairness in family travel.
